page.title: How to Hire a CFO in the UK
page.intro:
A CFO appointment is second only to CEO in strategic importance. The right CFO is a strategic partner to the CEO and board — not just a finance function head. They shape capital allocation, investor relationships, M&A strategy, and the financial architecture that underpins business performance. The candidate brief has become significantly more complex over the last decade.

Modern CFOs are expected to be commercially fluent, investor-ready, and digitally literate — as well as technically excellent. In PE-backed and listed businesses, the CFO is often the primary interface with the investment committee or institutional shareholders. In growth-stage businesses, the CFO is frequently the architect of the financial systems that make scale possible.

The profile of a CFO also varies significantly by sector. Regulated businesses (financial services, healthcare) require deep compliance knowledge. PE-backed businesses demand M&A experience and reporting discipline. Technology businesses value financial modelling and unit economics expertise. The brief must be sector-specific.
considerations: Define what CFO means for your organisation — the range from transactional finance head to strategic CFO is enormous, and the candidate profiles do not overlap.
PE-backed, listed, and private company requirements differ significantly — be explicit about the context and what investor or board relationships the CFO must manage.
Consider whether the CFO needs FCA/PRA registration or other regulatory credentials — this significantly narrows the candidate pool.
Assess the balance between external relationships (investors, banks, advisors) and internal focus — some CFOs excel at one but not both.
Succession context matters — if the CFO will be investor-facing for a future transaction, their track record in that context is essential.
page.salaryRange: £80,000–£350,000+ depending on sector, organisation size, and complexity
page.timeline: 12–16 weeks from briefing to accepted offer
page.whySearch: The pool of CFOs who combine commercial acumen, technical depth, and strong leadership credentials is smaller than most organisations realise. The best candidates are well-compensated, recognised by their peers, and receive multiple approaches every year. A headhunter with genuine sector relationships can identify who is actually available, who is performable, and how to structure an offer that will succeed.
